critical_issues: Stripe webhook handler is defined as both a Supabase Edge Function (Section 1.6 decision criteria table: 'Stripe webhook handler | Edge Function | Needs direct Supabase access, runs on Supabase infrastructure, deployed via Supabase CLI') AND as a Next.js API Route (Section 2.6 Payments endpoint table: 'POST /api/webhooks/stripe | Stripe webhook handler | Stripe signature | System'). These are fundamentally different deployment targets with different URLs. A Phase 7 developer following the API spec would build a Next.js route handler; a developer following Section 1.6 would build a Supabase Edge Function. This ambiguity must be resolved before build cards are created.
contradictions: D5 API grants course creation ('POST /api/courses') to both Operator and Senior Concierge roles. D3 F-025 specifies Senior Concierge has only 'read all except commission rates, update availability/contacts' — not course creation. Only Operator is granted 'full CRUD including commission/financial data' for course profiles. D2 Section 4.2 permission grid does not list a 'create course' permission for Senior Concierge either.

The representative Drizzle ORM schema translation for the 'clients' table (Section 1.13) omits several fields that exist in D4's DDL: phone_secondary, privacy_notice_version, membership_tier, network_sharing_consent_at, converted_from_lead_id, and converted_from_group_member_id. While labeled 'representative,' Phase 7 developers referencing this example would produce an incomplete schema, conflicting with D4's authoritative definition.

Stripe base rate is quoted as '2.9% + $0.30 MXN' in the Service Integration Matrix. D1 Section 6.3 quotes '2.9% + 30¢' without specifying MXN. For cross-border USD transactions from US cards, the flat fee denomination affects effective cost calculations. At $0.30 MXN (~$0.017 USD), this is substantially lower than the $0.30 USD cited in D1, creating a discrepancy in unit economics if interpreted differently.
warnings: Rate limiting uses in-memory state (Map) described as 'acceptable for single-deployment serverless.' Vercel's serverless and edge functions are stateless across invocations and instances do not share memory, making in-memory rate limiting ineffective in practice. While the document mentions 'Vercel KV if distributed needed,' the current design would not enforce rate limits reliably even with 2-4 users.

No API endpoint is defined for creating round_feedback records, despite the round_feedback table existing in D4 Section 2.6 and being listed in the D6 manifest's tables_referenced for the client history endpoint. There is no way to write feedback data into the system via the specified API.

Website Form Integration identified as an MVP integration in D3 Section 5.4 ('Webhook from the website form to the platform's lead creation API endpoint') is absent from D5's Service Integration Matrix (Part 3). While the POST /api/leads endpoint exists, the webhook receiver and its configuration are not specified.

Event-driven Edge Function triggers for booking status changes (e.g., 'Booking status → completed' triggering create-followup-tasks) lack specificity on the trigger mechanism. It is unclear whether these use Supabase database webhooks, direct HTTP calls from the API route to the Edge Function, or another pattern. Phase 7 build cards need a definitive answer.

Platform Owner role is included in the JWT claims structure (Section 1.3.4) but no mechanism is described for the 'dual hat' scenario explicitly defined in D2 Section 1.4 ('initially Rafael wearing a second hat alongside his Operator role'). The JWT can carry only one role, and there is no context-switching, secondary token, or special operator_id handling described for network-level aggregate access.

Decision Ledger entries D55-P5-016 through D55-P5-032 (17 entries) are described in a single summary paragraph rather than individually documented like D55-P5-001 through D55-P5-015. This reduces traceability for downstream phases that need to reference specific decisions about timezone handling, connection pooling, CORS policy, cookie inventory, etc.

The document does not address how the booking status change in a Next.js API Route (PUT /api/bookings/[id]/status) triggers the Supabase Edge Functions listed in Section 1.6 (create-followup-tasks, create-pretrip-tasks). The execution boundary between Vercel-hosted routes and Supabase-hosted Edge Functions needs an explicit bridging pattern.
